symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly worsening bilateral leg oedema extending to the thighs

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute decompensation of chronic kidney disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Salt and water retention from falling GFR produces progressive dependent oedema and weight gain.
Bilateral pitting oedema is shared with cardiac failure — non-discriminating on its own.
Symmetrical bilateral oedema argues strongly against a unilateral DVT.

symptoms.symptom_name: Uraemic symptom cluster — intractable nausea/vomiting, anorexia, generalised pruritus without rash, metallic taste

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute decompensation of chronic kidney disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nausea, pruritus without rash and metallic taste together are the classic uraemic constellation of advanced renal failure.
A uraemic cluster is not explained by cardiac failure and points to the renal driver.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known long-standing declining CKD on an ACE inhibitor (ramipril) and amlodipine

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ACE inhibitor use during acute renal deterioration and volume depletion from vomiting is a major driver of hyperkalaemia.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute decompensation of chronic kidney disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A known declining baseline establishes the acute-on-chronic frame.

symptoms.symptom_name: Weight gain of approximately 5 kg over one month (facility scales)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute decompensation of chronic kidney disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid weight gain quantifies fluid retention; patient/carer-obtainable so admissible in telehealth, unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Constitutional decline — profound fatigue/bedbound, pallor, restless legs and nocturnal muscle cramps

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute decompensation of chronic kidney disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pallor (anaemia of CKD), restless legs and cramps are recognised features of advanced uraemia and CKD-related electrolyte disturbance.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute decompensation of chronic kidney disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Impaired urinary concentrating ability is common in CKD but non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe hypertension (BP 175/100, carer-measured) with borderline-raised respiratory rate

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: hypertensive emergency / impending flash pulmonary oedema
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Volume-dependent severe hypertension with a rising respiratory rate signals threatened pulmonary oedema; carer-measured, admissible but unverified.

investigation_details.expected_result: Potassium potentially elevated (hyperkalaemia) with elevated urea/creatinine and low eGFR
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Occult hyperkalaemia missed — risk of fatal arrhythmia.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Only a venous potassium (± ECG) can confirm or exclude hyperkalaemia — unobtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: eGFR persistently <60 mL/min/1.73 m2 for >3 months

investigation_details.expected_result: eGFR <60 mL/min/1.73 m2, in keeping with established CKD, likely acutely lower
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity of the acute decompensation cannot be quantified.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute decompensation of chronic kidney disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Persistently reduced eGFR for >3 months defines CKD; an acute fall quantifies the decompensation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute decompensation of chronic kidney disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced erythropoietin production causes the normocytic normochromic anaemia of CKD; explains the pallor.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: hypertensive emergency / impending flash pulmonary oedema
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clear lungs on in-person auscultation lower the probability of established pulmonary oedema at this instant but do not exclude impending oedema; crucially this reassurance is a clinician finding the AI cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe 3+ pitting oedema to mid-thighs with tight shiny skin, and uraemic fetor (clinician exam)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute decompensation of chronic kidney disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Graded 3+ pitting oedema quantifies fluid overload and uraemic fetor confirms uraemia — both require a clinician's hands/senses and are sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No ascites, jaundice or stigmata of chronic liver disease

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of ascites/jaundice/stigmata makes cirrhotic oedema unlikely.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Uraemic syndrome on a background of fluid overload
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute decompensation of chronic kidney disease with fluid overload and uraemia
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of rapid fluid overload with a uraemic symptom cluster in known CKD is the core recognisable pattern and drives the urgent work-up.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent venous UEC with potassium and eGFR
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The pivotal test — quantifies renal function and detects/excludes hyperkalaemia; cannot be obtained via telehealth, which is precisely why in-person escalation is required.
Screens for hyperkalaemic changes while potassium is pending.
Characterises the anaemia of CKD and provides a baseline.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Chest examination / imaging for fluid status
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses respiratory reserve and pulmonary oedema — requires in-person clinician assessment.
